claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements complete boundary audit via SHA-256 file snapshot comparison before/after agent execution, with enforcement that converts any detected mutations into PRD readiness blockers via BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION code.

evidence.summary: fileDigest computes SHA-256 hash using createHash('sha256').update(readFileSync(path)).digest('hex'), confirming SHA-256 algorithm.
evidence.why: Confirms the exact hash algorithm used for snapshot integrity verification.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot resolves allowedRoots, uses gitFiles or walkFiles for file discovery, skips allowed roots via isWithin check, computes SHA-256 digests, returns Map.
evidence.why: Independently confirms explorer's claim about snapshot creation and allowed root exclusion logic.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots iterates before/after Maps, detects deleted, modified, and added files by comparing presence and digest equality, returns sorted changes array.
evidence.why: Verifies the core change detection logic that identifies mutations.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot(plan.project_root, [plan.output_dir]) executes BEFORE mkdirSync and agent loop, establishing baseline state.
evidence.why: Confirms pre-execution snapshot timing matches explorer's evidence.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot called AFTER agent loop completes at line 640, diffBoundarySnapshots computes changes stored in result.boundary with project_mutation and changes array.
evidence.why: Confirms post-execution audit and boundary result structure.

evidence.summary: boundaryBlockers maps changes to BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers; finalReadiness spreads readiness.blockers with runtimeBlockers and sets prd_ready false when blockers exist.
evidence.why: Verifies the enforcement mechanism that converts mutations into PRD readiness blockers.

evidence.summary: execution_policy sets project_mutation forbidden and allowed_write_roots [repoRelative(outputDir)], declaring boundary constraints.
evidence.why: Confirms policy configuration matches explorer's evidence.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt hard rules: 'do not modify target project files' and 'If verification would require mutation, report it in missing', confirming non-editing constraint.
evidence.why: Verifies agent protocol constraints on evidence gathering.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt requires 'Every evidence record must include scope: project, external, user, or unknown.' and 'Project facts require project-scoped evidence with repo-relative path.'
evidence.why: Confirms scope declaration and path requirements for project evidence.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues returns PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker when has_project_scope is true without path locator, or PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE when no project scope.
evidence.why: Independently verifies router validation logic for project evidence requirements.

evidence.summary: Agent results loop calls evidenceResultBlockingIssues, evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues, and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues; blocking issues pushed to blockerList.
evidence.why: Confirms agent result validation integration into readiness blockers.

evidence.summary: gitFiles uses git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z; walkFiles recursively reads directories excluding .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache.
evidence.why: Verifies file discovery methods and exclusion patterns for snapshot completeness.

evidence.summary: isWithin computes relative(path, root) and checks for absence of .. prefix and non-absolute path, confirming subtree containment logic.
evidence.why: Verifies allowed root exclusion logic prevents false positives from legitimate writes.

evidence.summary: finalReadiness conditional spreads readiness with [...readiness.blockers, ...runtimeBlockers] and prd_ready false when runtimeBlockers exist, confirming blocker enforcement.
evidence.why: Verifies that boundary mutations actually block PRD readiness.

assumptions: git ls-files -co --exclude-standard returns all tracked and untracked files excluding .gitignore patterns.
Agent provider filesystem operations are observable by buildBoundarySnapshot via standard file walking.
SHA-256 digests computed via readFileSync capture complete file content including any agent writes.
Agent provider cannot bypass snapshot detection by writing outside project root or using special filesystem features.
File system state remains stable between snapshot computation and agent execution for non-mutated files.
risks: If git ls-files misses untracked files not covered by walkFiles fallback, mutations could occur undetected.
If agent provider has direct block-device or volume-level filesystem access, snapshot could miss mutations.
If allowedRoots configuration includes project root subdirectories incorrectly, legitimate project mutations could be allowed.
If buildBoundarySnapshot excludes very large files or symlinks via fileDigest null return, those mutations would not be detected.

result.notes: Cross-check confirms all explorer claims via independent code verification. Boundary audit implementation exists with SHA-256 snapshot comparison, before/after timing, and BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker enforcement. Router validation integration confirmed. Assumptions align with filesystem and git behavior limitations.
